personas.name: Reflective Mood Tracker
personas.description: A user who relies on regular mood check-ins, journaling, and pattern recognition to better understand emotional wellbeing over time.
goals: Use journaling and check-ins for self-reflection
pain_points: Needs a structured way to log feelings
Wants meaningful reflection from tracked mood data
context: Uses the app multiple times a day for mood check-ins
May review past entries to understand recurring emotions
Values self-awareness and emotional pattern tracking
constraints: Mood entries should be easy to record regularly
Past mood data should remain accessible
The app should support repeated daily check-ins

personas.name: Stress and Emotional Support Seeker
personas.description: A user who turns to the app for help managing stress, emotional struggles, anxiety, or low mood.
goals: Use the app to better cope with anxiety or low mood
pain_points: Needs support that is practical and calming
May struggle with long or demanding interactions when stressed
context: Uses the app during emotionally difficult moments
May rely on the app as part of daily mental health support
Needs help that feels manageable during stress
constraints: Support features should be accessible during stressful moments
The app should not overwhelm the user with excessive input demands
The app should help users express emotional struggles clearly

personas.name: Insight and Information-Oriented User
personas.description: A user who values accurate mental health information, educational content, and app-generated insights.
goals: Learn more about mental health through the app
Use app information to support self-understanding
pain_points: Becomes frustrated when content lacks depth or usefulness
Needs information that feels trustworthy and relevant
May stop engaging if insights feel repetitive or vague
context: Uses the app for both tracking and learning
May compare app information to personal experience or professional advice
Values educational and informative app content
constraints: Information should be accessible within the app
Insights should be understandable and clearly presented
Educational content should feel relevant to the user’s needs

personas.name: Cost-Conscious Free User
personas.description: A user who is interested in the app’s mental health features but is frustrated by paywalls, subscriptions, or limited free access.
goals: Access useful features without needing to pay immediately
Understand the value of premium content
Use the free version effectively before committing to payment
pain_points: Too many features feel locked behind payment
Free version may feel too limited
Unclear whether premium content is worth the cost
context: May consider premium if value is clear
Is sensitive to subscription pricing and feature restrictions
constraints: Users should be able to distinguish free and paid content
Core free functionality should still feel useful
Premium restrictions should be communicated clearly

personas.name: Privacy and Trust Sensitive User
personas.description: A user who is highly concerned about personal data handling, privacy, and whether sensitive mental health information is shared appropriately.
goals: Use the app without fear of inappropriate data sharing
Trust the app with sensitive mental health information
Understand how personal information is handled
pain_points: Concerned about data being shared with third parties
May uninstall the app if privacy terms feel unsafe
Needs transparency around sensitive data handling
context: Uses the app for sensitive mental health tracking
May review privacy terms before continued use
Trust is critical to long-term engagement
constraints: Privacy information should be visible and understandable
Sensitive data handling should be transparent
Users should be able to assess trust before continuing use
